design_philosophy.core_loop: Select pitch type/location, time swing to make contact, manage pitcher stamina across innings, execute base running
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Timing-based batting and pitching mechanics balanced against pitcher fatigue management and opponent skill variation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead an MLB roster to victory using real player names and differentiated abilities; experience arcade-style baseball with licensed authenticity
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes arcade-style accessibility over simulation; implements mercy rule (10+ run lead ends game); prioritizes individual player ability differentiation (speed, power, control) over complex statistical simulation; all pitchers have varying stamina that directly impacts ball speed and steerability

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can select any starting pitcher regardless of recent use; ability to substitute pinch hitters at any position provides roster flexibility; opponent pitcher selection and player attribute variation creates natural difficulty scaling

progression.unlock_mechanism: 8 location-based teams (Boston, California, Detroit, Houston, Minnesota, New York, St. Louis, San Francisco) plus two All-Star rosters (American League and National League) available for selection
replayability_hooks: Different team rosters with varied player abilities encourage replays with different lineups
Pitcher stamina system requires strategic rotation across consecutive games
Individual player strengths (e.g., Vince Coleman's speed for stealing bases, Fernando Valenzuela's pitch movement) support different playstyle approaches

world_building_techniques: Licensed MLBPA player names ground game in real baseball context
Team rosters based on 1986-1987 playoff rosters provide historical authenticity
Player-specific statistics and abilities reference real-world performance characteristics

faction_indicators: 8 location-based team choices (Boston, California, Detroit, Houston, Minnesota, New York, St. Louis, San Francisco)
American League vs National League All-Star rosters provide opposing faction structure
danger_signals: High-speed pitchers (Nolan Ryan, Roger Clemens) represent pitcher threats
Pitcher stamina depletion across consecutive games creates strategic vulnerability
Specific batters with power (Mark McGwire, José Canseco) signal offensive threats
environmental_storytelling: Box score display tracks game progression and composite team statistics
Pitcher stamina degradation visible through reduced ball speed and control during gameplay
Team rosters reflect actual 1986-1987 playoff team compositions, anchoring game in historical moment
